hook.text: Massive jaw muscles, huge flat teeth, a bony skull crest — built to chew tough plants all day long.

buildup.text: Robust australopithecines (Paranthropus) had powerful chewing muscles anchored to a sagittal crest on the skull.

discovery.text: They specialized in hard, fibrous foods — seeds, roots, and tough plants that other hominins couldn't process.

twist.text: Specialization was their downfall. When climates shifted and food sources changed, they couldn't adapt and went extinct.

climax.text: Paranthropus shows that evolution has no guaranteed winners. Specialization is a gamble against future change.

punchline.text: The specialists lost; the generalists survived.

quiz.question: Why did robust australopithecines go extinct?
options: Their dietary specialization failed when climates changed
